variant_cover_1.concept: A precise technical cross-section of a bell's interior rendered as a scientific illustration, with standing wave geometries mapped inside the void like a cartographer charting grief.
variant_cover_1.medium: Copper-plate engraving style with single warm ink wash accent
variant_cover_1.prompt: Technical cross-section diagram of a massive cathedral bell rendered in meticulous copper-plate engraving style, the bell bisected vertically to reveal its interior void mapped with concentric standing wave geometries — perfect arcs and nodes labeled in a fine antiquarian hand. The metal itself shown in geological strata layers, bronze alloy compositions annotated with hairline leader lines. Monochrome ink black on aged limestone-white paper, the single accent being a pool of warm tallow-amber light pooling at the bell's lowest interior point, as if something molten still rests there. Dürer-precise cross-hatching, no human figures, the diagram's clinical objectivity unable to conceal that its subject is a throat. No text, no title, no lettering. Pure illustration.

variant_cover_2.concept: A boy's hands seen from directly above, resting palm-up on a stone workbench covered in bronze filings, a pitch-fork, and a single folded letter — the hands too still, the stillness saying everything.
variant_cover_2.medium: Photorealistic oil painting, shallow depth of field, single candle source
variant_cover_2.prompt: Extreme overhead close-up oil painting of two adolescent hands lying open and upward on a cold limestone workbench, rendered with photorealistic precision in warm tallow-candle amber light from a single off-frame source. The bench surface scattered with fine bronze filings catching the light like rust-gold dust, a tuning fork resting at the frame's edge, and a half-unfolded letter whose handwriting is just illegible. Deep shadow fills the background; the hands are the only warmth in the composition. Shallow depth of field softens the letter's edges but keeps the hands in devastating focus — the skin slightly too rough for fourteen, the palms slightly too empty. No text, no title, no lettering. Pure illustration.

variant_cover_3.concept: The novel's title constructed entirely from the interference patterns of two overlapping sound waves, the letterforms emerging from resonance geometry rather than being drawn.
variant_cover_3.medium: Pure vector geometry, black on cream, no illustration
variant_cover_3.prompt: Purely typographic and geometric cover: the title letterforms built from the mathematically accurate interference patterns of two overlapping circular wave systems, the letters emerging where wave crests align — visible only in the constructive interference zones, dissolving back into wave geometry at their edges. Cold ink black on aged parchment cream, no illustration, no figurative elements whatsoever. The wave-source points are marked with small precise crosses like a physics diagram. A single thin horizontal rule at one-third height grounds the composition. The overall effect is of a word trying to cohere out of vibration, almost succeeding. No text, no title, no lettering. Pure illustration.

variant_cover_4.concept: A vast limestone bowl-city seen from its rim at blue pre-dawn, impossibly miniaturized and detailed, the single bell tower rising as the only vertical in a horizontal world of curved stone.
variant_cover_4.medium: Watercolor with fine ink linework overlay, Piranesi spatial scale
variant_cover_4.prompt: Panoramic watercolor landscape of an entire city built inside a natural limestone bowl, viewed from the curved rim's edge looking down and inward, rendered in Piranesi's vertiginous spatial scale with exhaustive architectural detail achieved through fine ink linework over pale washes. Pre-dawn light: the sky a cold resonance blue darkening to ink at the zenith, the stone city below in deep pewter shadow except where a single bell tower catches the first gray light and becomes the image's sole vertical element in a composition of curved horizontals. The city is fully inhabited — laundry, market stalls, a thousand windows — but entirely still, not a person visible. Dried-blood rust bleeds into the limestone shadows where moisture meets old iron. No text, no title, no lettering. Pure illustration.

variant_cover_5.concept: The moment of a bell's casting as pure abstract expressionism — molten bronze meeting cold mold as a collision of warm and cold color fields, the violence of transformation rendered as geometry.
variant_cover_5.medium: Reduction linocut, three-color, architectural geometry meets gestural mark
variant_cover_5.prompt: Three-color reduction linocut print: the abstract collision of two geometric systems — a descending form of precise architectural arcs in cold pewter and verdigris representing the mold, a rising eruption of warm amber-orange and dried-blood rust representing the pour of molten bronze. Where they meet at the center of the composition, the two systems interpenetrate and neither wins. No bell shape is explicitly depicted; the bell exists only as the negative space between the two forces. Heavy linocut texture throughout, tool marks visible in the ink, the geometry disciplined but the color boundaries slightly bleeding — precision that cannot fully contain what it is trying to contain. No text, no title, no lettering. Pure illustration.

variant_cover_6.concept: A long stone corridor of the foundry seen in forced perspective, every bell in storage hung in perfect institutional rows, and one empty hook at the corridor's vanishing point where the largest bell should be.
variant_cover_6.medium: Ink wash with obsessive fine-line architectural drafting, Shaun Tan emotional register
variant_cover_6.prompt: Deep forced-perspective ink wash illustration of an underground foundry storage corridor stretching to a precise vanishing point, both walls hung with bells in graduated sizes — smallest at the foreground, largest toward the back — each bell numbered and tagged in a bureaucratic hand, their arrangement too perfect, the institution's pride made visible. The corridor's stone is rendered with Piranesi's architectural obsession, every block cross-hatched in Dürer's discipline. At the vanishing point: one empty hook. The largest bell is missing. The hook's shadow falls as if the bell were still there. No figures. The palette is almost entirely cold pewter and limestone off-white, with only the empty hook warmed by a single amber light source above it, illuminating nothing. No text, no title, no lettering. Pure illustration.

variant_ornament_1.concept: A precise cross-section diagram of a bell rendered as if from an 18th-century acoustician's manual. The bell's interior contains standing wave patterns drawn as concentric arcs with amplitude markings. Fine hatching defines the bronze wall thickness. Changes per chapter: wave nodes shift position, multiply, or collapse into a single line.
variant_ornament_1.medium: Fine engraving line work, black ink on white, no fill — pure contour and cross-hatching
variant_ornament_1.prompt: A small centered chapter ornament, maximum 2 inches wide, rendered in fine engraving style: a precise technical cross-section of a church bell shown in bilateral symmetry, walls defined by parallel hatching lines suggesting bronze density, the hollow interior filled with standing wave arcs drawn as thin concentric curves with evenly spaced amplitude crests, nodal points marked with small crosses as in an acoustic diagram, a hairline baseline beneath, all on white ground, no tonal wash, only incised-looking black linework of extreme precision and quietude White background. No text.

variant_ornament_2.direction: Muted Ink Wash Silhouette with Ghost Geometry
variant_ornament_2.concept: A bell rendered almost entirely as a soft gray ink wash silhouette — atmospheric, slightly grieving in tone — with only the standing wave pattern drawn in sharp black line over the wash, as if the physics persists after the form dissolves. The bell shape blurs at edges; the waves are the only hard truth.
variant_ornament_2.medium: Watercolor ink wash for bell body, crisp black ruling pen lines for wave geometry — two-material tension
variant_ornament_2.prompt: A small centered chapter ornament, maximum 2 inches wide: a bell shape rendered as a diffuse gray ink wash silhouette, edges soft and bleeding slightly as if dampened paper, the form barely holding its outline, overlaid with precisely ruled thin black lines describing standing wave arcs inside the bell's cavity — the waves sharper and more real than the bell itself, nodal points where lines cross rendered as tiny solid dots, the wash and the geometry in deliberate visual conflict, white page ground, no border, melancholic and technical simultaneously White background. No text.

variant_ornament_3.concept: The bell reduced to a single unbroken contour line of variable weight — almost a glyph. Inside: only one standing wave rendered as a single arc. Absolute minimalism. Per chapter, the wave arc changes curvature: tight and high-frequency early in the book, loosening and finally becoming a straight horizontal line at the novel's end.
variant_ornament_3.medium: Single-weight brush or ruling pen line, pure black on white — approaching calligraphic abstraction
variant_ornament_3.prompt: A small centered chapter ornament, maximum 2 inches wide: a bell form drawn as a single continuous contour line of uniform fine weight, closed and symmetrical, containing in its interior exactly one standing wave arc rendered as a single clean curve — the arc sits centered in the bell's hollow, touching neither wall, drawn with the same line weight as the outline, no hatching, no fill, no additional marks, maximum negative space, the ornament approaching the simplicity of a mathematical symbol, black on white, perfectly centered White background. No text.

variant_ornament_4.concept: The bell built entirely from stippled dots — thousands of small points creating deep shadow in the bronze thickness and luminous void in the cavity. The standing waves are rendered not as lines but as regions of dot-density variation: denser dots at pressure antinodes, open white space at nodes. Sound made visible as matter density.
variant_ornament_4.medium: Stipple engraving — no lines, only dots of varying density, black ink on white
variant_ornament_4.prompt: A small centered chapter ornament, maximum 2 inches wide, in pure stipple engraving technique: a bell cross-section where the bronze walls are built from dense accumulations of fine black dots creating rich dark tone, the interior cavity shown as lighter stipple that varies in dot density to describe standing wave pressure zones — antinodes appear as denser dot clusters forming arc-shaped bands, nodes as open near-white passages, the whole image constructed from dots alone with no ruling lines, high contrast between thick bell walls and resonant interior, centered on white page, no outline, form emerging purely from pointillist accumulation White background. No text.

variant_ornament_5.direction: Woodcut Bold Contrast with Folk-Sacred Hybrid
variant_ornament_5.concept: The bell rendered in woodcut aesthetic — high contrast, bold black masses, deliberately rough cut edges suggesting hand-carved wood. The bell takes on an iconic, almost heraldic quality. Standing waves become bold concentric black arcs with white gaps between them, like tree rings or a saint's halo reimagined as acoustic phenomenon. Primal and symbolic.
variant_ornament_5.medium: Woodcut aesthetic — thick black masses, white knife-cut lines, high contrast, no midtones
variant_ornament_5.prompt: A small centered chapter ornament, maximum 2 inches wide, in woodcut print aesthetic: a bell form rendered as bold black silhouette with white areas carved away to show wall thickness and interior, the standing wave pattern inside depicted as thick concentric black arcs separated by white gaps suggesting bold incised cuts, the wave arcs radiating from the bell's apex like a compressed halo, edges of the bell shape slightly irregular as if carved by a knife in soft wood, no fine detail, maximum contrast between black mass and white ground, the ornament reading as icon or seal, centered, powerful and abbreviated White background. No text.

variant_ornament_6.concept: The bell rendered as a genuine architectural or engineering blueprint excerpt — pale blue-white ground with white linework, or alternatively white ground with precise blue-gray lines. Dimension annotations, section markers, and wave frequency labels appear in tiny serif type. The ornament looks torn or cropped from a larger technical document. Each chapter shows a different frequency annotation.
variant_ornament_6.medium: Blueprint drafting aesthetic — fine white or blue-gray lines, annotation text, technical drawing conventions
variant_ornament_6.prompt: A small centered chapter ornament, maximum 2 inches wide, styled as a fragment of an architectural or acoustic engineering drawing: a bell cross-section drawn in fine blue-gray lines on white ground using precise drafting conventions, walls shown with double-line construction indicating material thickness, interior containing standing wave arcs drawn as light dashed curves with small frequency notation numbers in tiny serif type beside each arc, center axis shown as a fine dash-dot line, dimension tick marks on the bell's exterior, a partial title block visible at lower edge as if cropped from a larger sheet, the whole image suggesting a document excerpt, technical and quietly mournful, centered on white page White background. No text.

variant_map_1.direction: Traditional Parchment Fantasy Map — Full Color, Heavily Labeled
variant_map_1.concept: Cantamura rendered as a classical cartographer's commission, the kind a wealthy merchant house would hang in a counting room — warm ochres and burnt siennas, compass roses, decorative cartouches — but subtly wrong in the way that acoustic cities are wrong: the streets curve in arcs rather than grids, district borders follow contour lines of sound rather than walls or rivers, and the forty-seven bell towers are marked with individual heraldic symbols instead of uniform icons. Every label is present and legible. The Academy of Civic Pitch sits at map-center in gold leaf. Veth occupies the northeastern cold margin, labeled in faded ink as if added by a different hand, with the single annotation REMEMBERS in small Roman capitals beneath a smudged cartouche that was never finished.
variant_map_1.medium: Aged parchment with visible foxing and vellum texture, warm watercolor washes in ochre, burnt sienna, and verdigris, brown ink linework with quill-variation strokes, gold leaf highlights on the Academy and the original Bellwright forge site, decorative border featuring bell motifs and standing wave flourishes rendered as acanthus-leaf equivalents
variant_map_1.prompt: Fantasy cartography map of Cantamura, a city built inside a natural amphitheater, full color aged parchment style, classical 17th-century cartographic aesthetic with warm ochre and burnt sienna watercolor washes, brown quill-line streets that arc in acoustic curves rather than grids, forty-seven individually marked bell tower positions each with a unique heraldic glyph, the Academy of Civic Pitch at map center highlighted in gold leaf, district borders drawn as concentric sound-propagation arcs rather than walls, the Bellwright founding forge marked with a bronze medallion symbol, street names written in flowing italic script alongside their resonant frequency designations in smaller text, the natural amphitheater bowl rendered with careful topographic hachuring, Veth at the map's cold northeastern edge labeled in visibly faded ink by a different hand with the word REMEMBERS in small capitals beneath an unfinished cartouche, decorative compass rose incorporating tuning fork imagery, border ornamentation of intertwined bell silhouettes and standing wave patterns rendered as acanthus flourishes, overall warm and authoritative but with subtle wrongness in the geometry, high detail, museum-quality fantasy cartography

variant_map_2.direction: Acoustic Scientific Diagram — Black and White, Symbolically Coded
variant_map_2.concept: Cantamura as it would appear in an eighteenth-century natural philosophy instrument manual — the city reduced entirely to its acoustic logic, stripped of decorative sentiment. Clean copperplate engraving lines over white. The forty-seven bell positions are numbered nodes in a network diagram, connected by propagation vectors with precise angle notations. Concentric interference rings radiate from each node and overlap in mathematically rendered moiré patterns where they interact — these overlapping zones are the districts, defined by their interference character rather than by walls or names. The Academy sits at the point of maximum constructive interference, marked only as NODE CONVERGENCE. Streets are single lines labeled exclusively with frequency values in Hz. Veth does not appear on the diagram proper. At the bottom margin, in the space where an instrument manual would print calibration notes, a single line reads: VETH — FREQUENCY UNKNOWN. INSTRUMENT CANNOT RESOLVE.
variant_map_2.medium: Black ink only on white, copperplate engraving simulation with fine cross-hatching for topographic depth in the amphitheater bowl, geometric precision throughout, no color, no wash, pure linework with varied stroke weights distinguishing structural geography from acoustic propagation data, printed diagram aesthetic with numbered figure callouts
variant_map_2.prompt: Technical scientific diagram of Cantamura rendered as an 18th-century natural philosophy instrument manual plate, black ink engraving on white, no color, copperplate line aesthetic with fine cross-hatching, the city reduced to its acoustic logic entirely, forty-seven bell tower positions rendered as numbered network nodes connected by precisely angled propagation vectors, concentric interference rings radiating from each node overlapping in mathematically accurate moiré interference patterns, the overlap zones defining city districts with no names only interference-character annotations, the Academy of Civic Pitch at the point of maximum constructive interference labeled NODE CONVERGENCE with a double-circle symbol, all streets rendered as single ruled lines labeled exclusively with frequency values in Hz typography, the natural amphitheater bowl shown through precise topographic hachure lines in the engraving tradition, no decorative elements anywhere, numbered figure callouts in the margins referencing a legend that classifies interference types, at the bottom calibration-note margin a single line reading VETH — FREQUENCY UNKNOWN — INSTRUMENT CANNOT RESOLVE, overall aesthetic of a scientific instrument manual plate, rigorous geometric precision, pure black and white linework, symbolically coded throughout with no conventional map labels

variant_map_3.direction: Bird's-Eye Illustration — Full Color, Impressionistic Detail
variant_map_3.concept: Cantamura seen from directly above at an angle suggesting a bird hovering at the amphitheater's rim — a painterly, almost theatrical illustration rather than a map, the kind that would serve as a frontispiece in a city history or appear as a full-page plate in an illustrated novel. The acoustic geometry is felt rather than measured: the bells are visible as physical objects in their towers, the sound they produce rendered as luminous standing-wave geometry hanging in the air above the streets like visible weather, tinted in cool blues and pale golds. The city is alive — figures in the streets, smoke from forges, the Academy's windows lit from within. Veth is visible at the cold edge of the illustration as a district where the luminous wave-geometry simply stops, the air above it flat and dark, the streets there rendered in noticeably colder, grayer palette. No labels anywhere. The forty-seven bell towers are the only navigation.
variant_map_3.medium: Full color gouache and ink illustration, warm amber city tones contrasted with cool luminous blue-white standing wave geometry rendered as visible light above the streets, the Veth district in desaturated gray-blue, bird's-eye perspective with slight isometric tilt, painterly and atmospheric, impressionistic detail on architecture with precise detail only on the bells and the Academy
variant_map_3.prompt: Bird's-eye view full color fantasy city illustration of Cantamura seen from above at a slight isometric angle as if hovering at the amphitheater's natural rim, painterly gouache and ink style, warm amber and ochre city architecture filling the natural bowl of the amphitheater, forty-seven bell towers visible as physical bronze structures throughout the city each slightly different in form, standing wave geometry rendered as luminous pale blue-gold light patterns hanging visibly in the air above the streets like frozen sound made physical, cool luminous acoustic geometry contrasting with warm city architecture below, the Academy of Civic Pitch at the convergence point of multiple wave patterns glowing from within its windows, the streets curving in acoustic arcs visible from above, city alive with tiny figures, forge smoke, market activity, the Bellwright founding site marked by the oldest and largest bell tower at the amphitheater's original center, Veth district at the cold northeastern edge where the luminous wave geometry simply terminates abruptly, the air above Veth flat and dark, the architecture there rendered in desaturated gray-blue palette noticeably colder than the rest of the city, no text labels anywhere on the image, no compass rose, no legend, pure illustration, impressionistic architectural detail throughout with precise loving detail reserved only for the bells and the Academy, atmospheric depth with the amphitheater walls rising at the image edges, full color frontispiece illustration quality

variant_map_4.direction: Schematic Sepia Diagram — Unlabeled Except for Frequency Notations, Minimal
variant_map_4.concept: A working document — the kind that would be found folded in a legal scholar's coat pocket or pinned above a clerk's desk in the Registry of Tones. Sepia ink on cream paper, minimal and functional, stripped of beauty entirely. The city is rendered as a pure schematic: the amphitheater as an ellipse, the forty-seven bells as small filled circles at their precise positions, the streets as thin lines with no names, the districts as lightly shaded zones differentiated only by a hatching pattern key in the corner. The only text on the entire document is frequency notation — numbers, ratios, interval names in the Bellwright notation system — placed at each bell node and along the major acoustic corridors. The Academy is the only structure given a footprint rather than a dot. Veth is present as a zone where the hatching pattern has been crossed out and redrawn three times, the frequency notations beside it replaced with a handwritten question mark in slightly different ink, as if added later by someone who checked and was not satisfied.
variant_map_4.medium: Sepia ink on cream paper, schematic and functional, no wash, thin uniform linework except for slightly heavier strokes on the amphitheater perimeter and the Academy footprint, hatching patterns for district zones, handwritten frequency notations in a period-appropriate technical hand, the question mark beside Veth in slightly darker fresher ink suggesting it was added after the original document was completed
variant_map_4.prompt: Schematic working document map of Cantamura in sepia ink on cream paper, minimal and functional aesthetic, no decorative elements, the natural amphitheater rendered as a clean ellipse with slightly heavier perimeter line, forty-seven bell tower positions as small filled circles precisely placed within the ellipse, streets as thin uniform sepia lines with no street names, city districts as lightly differentiated zones using only hatching pattern variations explained by a small corner key, the Academy of Civic Pitch as the single structure given an architectural footprint outline rather than a dot, all text on the document consisting exclusively of frequency notation — hertz values, musical interval ratios, and Bellwright notation system designations — placed at each bell node and along the major acoustic corridor streets, no conventional map labels anywhere, no district names, no personal names, purely technical frequency data as the only language, Veth district present at the document's northeastern zone with hatching pattern visibly crossed out and redrawn in slightly overlapping strokes suggesting revision, the frequency notations beside Veth replaced with a handwritten question mark in noticeably darker fresher ink implying it was added by a different hand after the original document was finished, overall aesthetic of a functional working reference document not meant to be beautiful, sepia monochrome, schematic precision, the quiet unease of a document that almost accounts for everything
